Package cli

Provides a robust, strategy-based engine for parsing and validating command-line arguments in Java 8+ environments.

See: Description

  • Interface Summary 
    Interface Description
    FlagHandler
    Strategy interface for processing different types of command-line flags.
    FlagListener
    Defines a contract for observing and reacting to flag discovery events during the parsing process.
  • Class Summary 
    Class Description
    CommandFlagParser
    This is the core access point for orchestrating the command-line parsing operations for integration in your Java console-based development projects.
    FlagRegistry
    A central repository for all registered FlagRule definitions.
    FlagRule
    A stateful POJO (Plain Old Java Object) designed to represent a specific command-line flag definition and its subsequent parsed state.
    LexicalCommandProcessor
    Acts as a lexical pre-processor that transforms raw, fragmented command-line arguments into a sequence of cohesive, logical tokens.
    LongFlagHandler
    Provides a concrete strategy for processing long-form flags based on GNU conventions.
    ShortFlagHandler
    Provides a concrete strategy for processing short-form flags based on POSIX conventions.
    Tester  
  • Enum Summary 
    Enum Description
    FlagType
    Defines the expected behaviour and syntactical requirements for flag arguments and value separators.

Package cli Description

Provides a robust, strategy-based engine for parsing and validating command-line arguments in Java 8+ environments.

Key Architectural Pillars

  • Lexical Amalgamation: A pre-processing layer that heals fragmented shell inputs and handles complex comma-delimited data.
  • The Negative Number Shield: A regex-driven protection layer ensuring numeric data and ranges are not misidentified as command flags.
  • Observer-Driven Discovery: Utilises the FlagListener interface to decouple token extraction from state management.

Compliance

The package follows POSIX conventions for short flags (e.g., -vh) and GNU conventions for long flags (e.g., --verbose), supporting both attached values and explicit separators.
Since:
28 February 2026
Version:
2.0
Author:
Trevor Maggs
